name: Cocoa Cake
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup (1 1/2 sticks) margarine at room temperature
1/2 cup liquid sugar substitute equal to 1/2 cup sugar
3 large egg whites, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together cocoa and boiling water to blend. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
2. Cream margarine at medium speed until light and fluffy.
3. Add liquid sugar substitute, vanilla extract, cooled cocoa mixture, and remaining ingredients to the creamed mixture. Mix at medium speed until well blended.
4. Beat egg whites at medium speed until foamy. Gradually add cream of tartar and sugar, beating at high speed to form a meringue. Set aside for later use.
5. Stir together flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon in a separate bowl.
6. Add 1 cup cool water to the creamed mixture with the dry ingredients. Mix at medium speed until well blended.
7. Carefully stir batter into greased pans lined with wax paper on the bottom.
8. Bake at 350 degrees F for 30-35 minutes, or until a cake tester comes out clean from the center and the cake pulls away from the sides of the pan.
9. Turn cake out onto a cooling rack and remove wax paper. Cool to room temperature.
10. Spread diabetic jelly between cake layers and frost with fluffy frosting at the last minute.
11. Cut into 16 equal servings.
